Description: Conduct creative brainstorming sessions to generate initial game concept, setting, and theme ideas.

Task Name: Define Unique Selling Points (USPs) and Core Theme
Description: Identify the game’s unique selling points and establish the core theme and genre as guiding pillars for development.
Compile a pitch deck with the game overview, key features, early art mock-ups, and market analysis to pitch to stakeholders.
Dependencies: Define Unique Selling Points (USPs) and Core Theme

Description: Present the game concept and pitch deck to stakeholders/executives for greenlighting and initial feedback.
Refine the game concept and pitch deck based on feedback from the initial presentation to ensure buy-in.
Create a structured outline for the Game Design Document, covering gameplay, story, characters, levels, and technical requirements.
Write the initial draft of core gameplay mechanics (combat, movement, progression systems) in the GDD.
Draft the game’s story summary, world background, and setting details for inclusion in the GDD.
Create detailed profiles and backstories for main characters and notable NPCs for the GDD.
List various enemy types and outline their behaviors, attack styles, and roles in the game.
Outline each game level or area, including its theme, objectives, and how it fits into overall progression.
Detail systems such as health, experience, loot, and difficulty scaling in the GDD.

Task Name: Review GDD Draft with Department Leads
Description: Circulate the draft GDD to leads in art, programming, audio, and QA for cross-discipline feedback.

Task Name: Revise and Finalize Game Design Document
Description: Integrate feedback from all departments and publish the final version of the Game Design Document.
Dependencies: Review GDD Draft with Department Leads

Description: Analyze GDD to list technical requirements, engine capabilities needed, and potential development challenges.

Task Name: Select Game Engine and Development Tools
Description: Evaluate and choose a game engine (e.g., Unreal Engine) and key development tools (source control, build systems).
Install and configure the game engine and development tools for the team, including version control setup.
Dependencies: Select Game Engine and Development Tools

Task Name: Establish Code Standards and Repo Structure
Description: Define coding guidelines and organize the source control repository structure for the project.
Implement a basic prototype demonstrating character movement (running, jumping) and camera control.
Dependencies: Select Game Engine and Development Tools

Description: Create a prototype to test core combat mechanics like basic attacks, combos, and target locking.
Dependencies: Select Game Engine and Development Tools

Description: Prototype a simple enemy AI to chase and attack the player, testing pathfinding and basic combat AI.
Ensure physics interactions (ragdoll on enemy death, environment collision) work as expected in a small test scene.
Evaluate prototypes (movement, combat, AI, physics) for fun factor and technical viability; decide on next steps.
Dependencies: Basic Movement Prototype; Combat Mechanics Prototype; Enemy AI Behavior Prototype; Physics and Collision Prototype

Task Name: Art Style Research and Reference Gathering
Description: Collect visual references and inspirations for the game’s art style, including color palettes, lighting, and mood.
Create concept art illustrations exploring designs for the main character, including outfits and silhouettes.
Dependencies: Art Style Research and Reference Gathering

Description: Design concept art for the main antagonist or boss character, establishing a strong villain visual identity.
Dependencies: Art Style Research and Reference Gathering

Description: Paint a concept piece for the first level’s environment to set the tone for world design (e.g., village or forest).
Dependencies: Art Style Research and Reference Gathering

Description: Sketch designs for key enemy archetypes (grunt, ranged, heavy, etc.), aligning with the game's theme.
Compile a visual style guide that outlines the overall art direction: character style, environment style, UI style, and lighting.
Dependencies: Concept Art – Main Protagonist; Concept Art – Environment (Level 1)

Description: Review and approve the art style guide with creative leadership to ensure consistency across all assets.
Create top-down sketches or 3D blockouts of early level concepts to establish space and flow without details.
Identify all core gameplay mechanics (platforming, puzzles, stealth, etc.) beyond combat that will be in the game.
For each identified gameplay mechanic, conduct a feasibility analysis or quick prototype to ensure it’s doable.
Gather the leads of each department for a kickoff meeting to outline development methodologies and communication channels.
Mark the completion of pre-production; all concept documentation and initial planning tasks are done. Transition to production.
Dependencies: Revise and Finalize Game Design Document; Prototype Evaluation and Review; Art Style Guide Approval

Description: Design the complete layout for Level 1, including environment features, enemy placement, and objectives.
Design the layout for a forest village level, balancing exploration areas and combat encounters.
Create the layout for an ancient ruins level with vertical exploration and puzzle elements.
Outline the layout for a mountain pass level featuring narrow paths and ambush points.
Design the approach to the enemy castle including outer defenses, moat, and gate puzzles.

Task Name: Full Level Design – Level 6 (Throne Room Finale)
Description: Plan the final level with the throne room where the final boss fight takes place; include arena layout and traps.
Play through blockouts of all levels with the design team to ensure flow and fun; iterate layouts as needed.
Dependencies: Full Level Design – Level 1; Full Level Design – Level 6 (Throne Room Finale)

Description: Begin coding the core combat system in the engine, supporting light attacks, heavy attacks, and combos.
Add a lock-on feature to allow players to focus on specific enemies during combat.
Develop mechanics for ranged attacks or abilities (if applicable, like a bow or magic) usable by the player.
Refine the player movement and action controller to handle all traversal (running, jumping, dodging) smoothly.

Task Name: Implement Combo System and Skill Trees
Description: Code the system for unlocking and executing combo moves and player skill progression/trees.
Integrate melee, ranged, and combo systems and test together in a sandbox level to ensure they work in harmony.
Dependencies: Implement Target Lock-On System; Implement Ranged Combat Mechanics; Implement Combo System and Skill Trees

Description: Set up the basic AI framework/state machine for enemies (idle, patrol, chase, attack, flee states).
Program enemy attacks, defenses, and special moves; ensure each enemy type behaves according to design.
Develop AI for the final boss, including multiple phases and unique attack patterns.
If applicable, code AI for NPC allies or companions, enabling basic cooperative combat behaviors.
Bake navigation meshes for levels and ensure all AI characters can navigate the environment properly.
Implement a system to adjust enemy toughness or spawn rates based on player performance.
Develop a system for spawning enemies into levels (triggered spawns, wave spawns, etc.).
Test large numbers of enemies and NPCs in a level to ensure AI performance and pathfinding remain stable.
Dependencies: Implement Enemy AI Framework; AI Pathfinding and Navigation Meshes

Description: Create a detailed high-poly 3D model of the main character based on concept art.
Create a game-ready low-poly model from the high-poly sculpt and texture it (diffuse, normal, specular maps).
Set up a skeleton for the main character model and skin (bind) the model to the rig for animation.
Model a common grunt enemy (mid-poly for in-game use) based on concept art.

Task Name: Texture and Rig Enemy Type A (Grunt)
Description: Apply textures to the grunt model and rig it for animations (shared rig if possible for similar enemies).
Model an enemy with ranged attacks (archer or mage) with distinctive silhouette.

Task Name: Texture and Rig Enemy Type B (Ranged)
Description: Texture the ranged enemy model and rig it for animation.
Model a heavier, more armored enemy type (e.g., a knight or brute).

Task Name: Texture and Rig Enemy Type C (Heavy Unit)
Description: Texture the heavy enemy and rig it for animation.
Create a 3D model for a mid-game boss, larger scale and more detail than standard enemies.
Texture the mini-boss model and rig it, ensuring the rig supports any unique anatomical features.
Develop the 3D model for the final boss, incorporating complex designs from concept art.

Task Name: Texture and Rig Final Boss Character
Description: Texture the final boss with high-quality maps and rig it for multi-phase animations.
Create base models for NPC villagers with a few variations (clothing, faces) to populate friendly areas.
Texture these NPC models and rig them with basic skeletons (possibly using simplified human rig).
Model trees, rocks, foliage, and ground textures for the forest environments.
Create texture sets for forest assets (bark, leaves, rock surfaces) and apply to models.
Build models for village structures (huts, fences, carts) and props (barrels, market stalls).
Texture the village structure models with wood, straw, and fabric materials consistent with art style.
Create models for ancient ruins (broken pillars, statues, debris) to populate ruin levels.
Texture the ruins assets, emphasizing aged stone, moss, and wear for ancient feel.
Model components for the castle interiors and exteriors (walls, throne, banners, chandeliers).
Texture castle models with stone, metal, and fabric materials, matching the game's medieval style.
Create models for player weapons (swords, shields, bows) and key enemy weapons.
Apply textures to all weapon models (metal, leather, wood surfaces) and ensure they fit art style.
Ensure weapons and equipment are properly set up to attach to character rigs (sockets in engine).
Define the style and pacing of animations (realistic, exaggerated, etc.), documenting for animators.
Create animations for main character movement: idle, walk, run, jump, dodge, climb.
Animate main character attacks (light, heavy, combo finishers), block, and hit reactions.
Animate enemy type A attacks and reactions (taking damage, dying).
Animate enemy type B and C attacks, including ranged attack animations and heavy strikes.
Create dramatic attack, movement, and special move animations for mini-boss and final boss.
Animate NPC villagers with simple gesturing (waving, talking, sitting) to bring towns to life.
Plan mocap sessions by selecting scenes/moves to capture, hiring actors, and prepping a shot list.
Prepare mocap equipment (suits, cameras, software) and calibrate the system for recording.
Record motion capture for key combat animations (sword swings, dodges, finishers) with stunt actors.
Clean up mocap data (remove noise, fix glitches) and convert to animation curves for use in-engine.
Apply processed mocap animations to character rigs in the engine and adjust blending/transitions.
Hand-animate any actions not captured in mocap (magic effects, creatures, etc.).
Decide on an overall audio direction: orchestral score vs. electronic, realistic vs. stylized sound effects.
Write and record the main theme music for the game that will be used in the title screen and key moments.
Compose background music for each level or area, tailored to the environment mood and pacing.
Record or source sound effects for combat (sword clings, hits, monster growls) and UI interactions (button clicks).
Capture ambient audio (wind in forest, village chatter, cave drips) to enhance environment atmosphere.
Hire voice actors for main characters, NPCs, and enemy sounds; schedule recording sessions.
Record all character dialogues and narrations according to the script.
Integrate music tracks, sound effects, and ambient loops into the game engine with appropriate triggers.
Dependencies: Record Sound Effects (Combat & UI); Record Ambient Sounds (Environments)

Description: Adjust volume levels and apply filters/reverb to ensure a balanced audio experience across music, SFX, and VO.
Design initial HUD mockups (health bars, stamina, enemy health, mini-map layout) to match game style.
Sketch concepts for main menu, pause menu, inventory, and skill tree interfaces.
Establish the UI style (fonts, colors, button styles) consistent with overall game art direction.
Dependencies: UI Concept – HUD Mockups; UI Concept – Menus and Inventory

Description: Implement the in-game HUD (health, stamina bars, mini-map) in the engine, scripting basic functionality.
Code and assemble the main menu, pause menu, and settings screens.
Implement inventory screens and skill tree interface, including drag-and-drop for equipment and skill point allocation.
Link up UI interactions with sound effects (button click, menu open/close sounds).

Task Name: UX Playtest (Menu Flow and HUD Clarity)
Description: Conduct a small usability test with team members on the menu flow and HUD clarity, gather feedback.
Dependencies: Implement HUD UI; Implement Menu UI

Description: Storyboard the key cutscenes (intro, mid-game, ending), detailing shots and camera angles for cinematics.
Model and texture any unique assets needed for cutscenes (special props, unique locations not in regular levels).
Use the engine’s cinematic tools to script the intro cutscene, triggering animations, camera, and VFX.
Script the final cutscene in the engine, ensuring proper sequence of boss defeat to ending narrative.
Sync recorded voiceover lines with cutscene animations and ensure proper lip-sync (if applicable).
Add special effects to cutscenes (dramatic lighting, particle effects for magic) and polish camera transitions.
Dependencies: Implement Cutscene in Engine – Intro; Implement Cutscene in Engine – Ending

Description: Play through story cutscenes and sequences to ensure narrative consistency and that VO matches subtitles.
Dependencies: Implement Cutscene in Engine – Intro; Record Voiceovers (Story Dialogue)

Description: Set a deadline where all core features must be integrated into the game; beyond this, focus is on refinement.
Dependencies: Implement Sound in Engine; Implement Menu UI; Implement Cutscene in Engine – Ending

Description: Ensure all major systems (combat, AI, UI, sound, cinematics) are integrated into a single build for testing.
Produce a first playable version of the game (alpha) and distribute internally for team playtesting.
Conduct an internal playtesting session focusing on early levels; gather feedback on gameplay and bugs.
Collect feedback/bug reports from playtest and categorize issues (gameplay, bugs, difficulty, etc.) for action.
Tweak enemy counts, placement, and puzzle difficulty in early levels based on playtest feedback.
Adjust combat parameters (animation timing, input responsiveness, damage output) to improve feel.
Improve AI decision-making and responsiveness; fix any alpha-phase AI bugs or weird behaviors.
Add any new combat moves or abilities identified during playtesting to enhance gameplay variety.
Profile game performance in alpha and optimize rendering (LOD adjustments, culling, shader optimizations).
Optimize asset loading and memory usage to reduce load times and prevent memory-related crashes.
If game has co-op or online, create a prototype to test basic connectivity and synchronization.
Develop the system to save and load game progress, including checkpoints and player stats.
Implement achievement tracking and triggers for platform achievements/trophies.
Refine blending between animations and fix any janky movements observed during playtesting.
Enhance particle effects for special abilities and magic attacks (if any) based on playtest feedback for clarity.
Fine-tune camera behavior (auto-adjust, avoid clipping into walls) based on player feedback.
Release a second iteration of the alpha build incorporating fixes and improvements for further testing.
Adjust XP requirements, leveling speed, and skill unlock pacing from early playtest data.
Tweak enemy health/damage for balance; ensure grunt enemies aren't too easy or too hard in early levels.
Review cutscenes in alpha build to ensure they trigger at correct times and have appropriate pacing.
Verify voiceover lines are triggering correctly and subtitles match VO, check for missing lines.
Confirm that alpha phase goals are met (all features in, major bugs addressed) and move to beta phase.
Dependencies: Alpha Build Iteration 2; Alpha Player Progression Balance

Description: QA team does a full playthrough to list all bugs (major and minor) to be fixed during beta.
Address all critical/blocker bugs (crashes, progression blockers) identified in the comprehensive bug sweep.
Address high priority issues (animation glitches, AI getting stuck, severe FPS drops) reported by QA.
Address lower priority bugs (texture pops, small UI misalignments, minor audio sync issues).
Fine-tune collision boundaries and physics interactions so players and enemies don't clip through objects.
Optimize game performance for final target platforms: frame rate stability, memory usage, and load times.
Test online play under heavy load conditions (if applicable) to ensure network code stability.
Improve or fix any visual issues: texture seams, lighting inconsistencies, pop-in, LOD transitions.
Complete final audio mix and mastering: ensure music, sound effects, and VO are balanced and clear.
After fixes, run a regression test pass to ensure no new issues were introduced and fixed bugs stay fixed.
Dependencies: Fix Critical Bugs (Beta); Fix Major Bugs (Beta)

Description: Test game balance from start to finish: difficulty curve, economy, and player progression now that most bugs are fixed.
Create the final ‘gold’ build candidate that could potentially be the release version if it passes all checks.
Any last-minute minor tweaks to animations or art assets that are quick and safe to do.
Design team does a final review of game balance and adjusts any glaring issues (too easy/hard spots).
Ensure the game meets all platform certification requirements (console compliance checklists, PC settings etc.).
Fix any issues found during platform compliance testing (e.g., save data handling, menu flow).
Finalize all marketing materials such as key art, screenshots, and gameplay clips for store pages and marketing.
Launch the official game website and social media channels with game info, development updates, and teaser content.
Schedule demo sessions or provide early builds to press and content creators to generate buzz.
Edit and produce the final game trailer highlighting story, gameplay, and visuals for launch marketing.
Add subtitles or localized text to the trailer for different languages/regions as needed.
Distribute the final trailer to all channels (YouTube, game sites, social media) and set up ads.
etc.)
Due Date: Submit the game to rating boards for classification and ensure all content meets necessary guidelines.

Description: Final review meeting to confirm all departments are go for launch, checklists completed.
Dependencies: Address Compliance Issues; Release Trailer Distribution

Description: Upon passing all tests and reviews, officially approve the Gold Master build for production/duplication.
Prepare a plan for day-one patch content, post-launch updates, and live support in case of issues.
Mark the project as ready for launch with Gold Master approved, transition team to support and post-launch activities.
